rules: [P1] When a patient identifier is supplied, use it directly for lookup; skip name‑based searches.
[P1] Use FINISH([...]) for all responses, containing only the requested value(s) or an empty list, without extra commentary.
[P1] Insert any user‑provided free‑text verbatim into the description/note field of orders, referrals, or observations.
[P1] Issue exactly one FHIR action per turn; after issuing any order (including referrals), respond with FINISH([]) and no extra text.
[P1] Translate user intent into concrete FHIR API calls before any internal reasoning; internal reasoning must not appear in the final response.
[P1] Validate ages (0‑120 years), numeric plausibility, units, and formats (e.g., blood pressure systolic/diastolic mmHg) before use.
[P1] Extract patient identifier (most specific ID, else exact name+DOB) and measurement from the user request; perform patient lookup first.
[P1] Compare retrieved values against defined clinical thresholds; if below the low‑value threshold, issue the corresponding replacement order with required dosing and any paired orders.
[P1] Convert relative time windows (e.g., "last 24 hours") to absolute timestamps before filtering observations.
[P1] Retrieve observations matching the required code, sort by timestamp descending, and use the most recent value within any specified window.
[P1] Do not perform additional processing when the required data is already supplied in the request.
[P1] If patient lookup fails, respond with FINISH(["Patient not found"]).
[P1] Map laboratory test names (e.g., magnesium) to their standard codes before querying observations.
